name: Mock Test - 09
instruction: The Question Paper contains three sections. Section A has 25 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section C has 6 questions. Attempt any 5 questions. All questions carry equal marks. There is no negative marking.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is not the characteristic of ionic solids? They are electrical conductors in solid-state. Brittle nature. Very strong forces of interactions. Anisotropic nature.
questions.question_html:
Which of the following is not the characteristic of ionic solids?

  1. They are electrical conductors in solid-state.
  2. Brittle nature.
  3. Very strong forces of interactions.
  4. Anisotropic nature.
questions.explanation: Ionic solids ions are not free to move so they act as electrical insulators in solid-state. So, the given statement is incorrect while ionic solids are anisotropic and brittle linked with a very strong force of interactions.

questions.question_text: If molality of aqueous solution of H 2 SO 4 is 2, then normality of solution whose density is 1.2 g/mL is
questions.question_html: If molality of aqueous solution of H2SO4 is 2, then normality of solution whose density is 1.2 g/mL is
questions.explanation: Mass of solution = mass of solute + solvent = 196+1000= 1196g Volume of solution {tex} = \frac{{1196}}{{1.2}} = 996.66ml{/tex} {tex}M = \frac{{2000}}{{996.66}} = 2.006\;M\;N = M \times 2 = 4.01N{/tex} = 1196/1.2=996.66 ml M=2000/996.66 = 2.006 M N= M {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 4.01N

questions.question_text: Which gas is evolved when the urea is treated with NaOH?
questions.question_html: Which gas is evolved when the urea is treated with NaOH?
questions.explanation: H 2 NCONH 2 + 2NaOH {tex}\to{/tex} 2NH 3 + Na 2 CO 3

questions.question_text: Arrange the following compounds in increasing order of their boiling points. CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 Br {tex}{{H}_{3}}C-\underset{Br}{\overset{C{{H}_{3}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\overset{|}{\mathop{C}}}\,}}}\,-C{{H}_{3}}{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Arrange the following compounds in increasing order of their boiling points.

  2. CH3CH2CH2CH2Br
  3. {tex}{{H}_{3}}C-\underset{Br}{\overset{C{{H}_{3}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\overset{|}{\mathop{C}}}\,}}}\,-C{{H}_{3}}{/tex}
questions.explanation: The bolling points of isomeric haloalkanes decrease with an increase in branching as with an increase in branching surface area decreases which leads to a decrease in intermolecular forces. Hence, the increasing order of their boiling points is c < a < b.

questions.question_text: One mole of an organic compound 'A' with the formula C 3 H 8 O reacts completely with two moles of HI to form X and Y. When 'Y' is boiled with aqueous alkali forms Z. Z answers the iodoform test. The compound 'A' is ______.
questions.question_html: One mole of an organic compound 'A' with the formula C3H8O reacts completely with two moles of HI to form X and Y. When 'Y' is boiled with aqueous alkali forms Z. Z answers the iodoform test. The compound 'A' is ______.
questions.explanation: Ether reacts with HI to form alcohol and alkyl iodide. Alcohol on oxidation will give the iodoform test.

questions.question_text: Protein is a
questions.question_html: Protein is a
questions.explanation: In proteins α-amino acids are connected to each other by peptide bond or peptide linkage. Chemically, peptide linkage is an amide formed between –COOH group and –NH 2 group. The reaction between two molecules of similar or different amino acids, proceeds through the combination of the amino group of one molecule with the carboxyl group of the other. This results in the elimination of a water molecule and formation of a peptide bond –CO–NH–. So protein is condensation polymer.

questions.question_text: Which type of solid conduct electricity in molten state but not in solid state?
questions.question_html: Which type of solid conduct electricity in molten state but not in solid state?
questions.explanation: In solid state the ions are not free to move about, hence they are electrical insulators. However, in molten state or when dissolved in water, the ions become free to move about and they conduct electricity.

questions.question_text: Benzylic halides contains:
questions.question_html: Benzylic halides contains:

options.text: a halogen atom bonded to an alkyl group.
options.html: a halogen atom bonded to an alkyl group.

options.text: sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring bonded to a halogen.
options.html: sp<sup>3</sup>-hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring bonded to a halogen.

options.text: sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.
options.html: sp<sup>3</sup>-hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.

options.text: sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom next to an aromatic ring.
options.html: sp<sup>2</sup>-hybridized carbon atom next to an aromatic ring.

questions.correct_option_text: sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring bonded to a halogen.
questions.explanation: Benzylic halides are the compounds in which the halogen atom is bonded to an sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom next to an aromatic ring. For example C 6 H 5 CH 2 Cl.

questions.question_text: [Ar]3d 10 4s 2 4p 3 is the electronic configuration of
questions.question_html: [Ar]3d104s24p3 is the electronic configuration of
questions.explanation: Arsenic is a group 15 element and its atomic no. is 33.

questions.question_text: Triiodomethane (Iodoform) is:
questions.question_html: Triiodomethane (Iodoform) is:
questions.explanation: The compound finds small scale use as a disinfectant. Around the beginning of the 20th century, it was used in medicine as a healing and antiseptic dressing for wounds and sores, although this use is now superseded by superior antiseptics.

questions.question_text: Decomposition of benzene diozonium chloride by using Cu 2 Cl 2 /HCl to form chlorobenzene is:
questions.question_html: Decomposition of benzene diozonium chloride by using Cu2Cl2/HCl to form chlorobenzene is:
questions.explanation: {tex}{C_6}{H_5}N_2^ + C{l^ - } + C{u_2}C{l_2}/HCl \to {C_6}{H_5}Cl{/tex} Mixing the solution of the freshly prepared diazonium salt with cuprous chloride or cuprous bromide results in the replacement of the diazonium group by –Cl or –Br. This is called Sandmeyer's reaction.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is not a globular protein?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is not a globular protein?
questions.explanation: Globular proteins are formed when the chains of polypeptides coil around to give a spherical shape. These are usually soluble in water. When the polypeptide chains run parallel and are held together by hydrogen and disulphide bonds, then fibre– like structure is formed. Such proteins called fibrous proteins are generally insoluble in water. Myosin (present in muscles), is a fibrous protein and not a globular protein.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is not true about the ionic solids?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is not true about the ionic solids?

options.text: Bigger ions form the close packed structure.
options.html: Bigger ions form the close packed structure.

options.text: Occupation of all the voids is not necessary.
options.html: Occupation of all the voids is not necessary.

options.text: The fraction of octahedral or tetrahedral voids occupied depends upon the radii of the ions occupying the voids.
options.html: The fraction of octahedral or tetrahedral voids occupied depends upon the radii of the ions occupying the voids.

options.text: Smaller ions occupy either the tetrahedral or the octahedral voids depending upon their size.
options.html: Smaller ions occupy either the tetrahedral or the octahedral voids depending upon their size.

questions.correct_option_text: The fraction of octahedral or tetrahedral voids occupied depends upon the radii of the ions occupying the voids.
questions.explanation: The fraction of octahedral or tetrahedral voids occupied depends upon the radii of the ions occupying the voids. Radii of the octahedral or tetrahedral void are related to radii of atoms (r) as The radius of the octahedral void (R 0 ) = 0.414 r The radius of the tetrahedral void (R 1 ) =0.225r where r = radius of bigger atom involved.

questions.question_text: Amount of NaOH required to neutarlize 1.5 L of 0.1 N HCl
questions.question_html: Amount of NaOH required to neutarlize 1.5 L of 0.1 N HCl
questions.explanation: Mass of NaOH = (1.5 {tex} \times {/tex} 0.1) {tex} \times {/tex} 40 = 6

questions.question_text: Which among the following forms basic oxide?
questions.question_html: Which among the following forms basic oxide?
questions.explanation: As we move down the group in the periodic table, the metallic character increases so Bi is a metal thus its oxide is basic.

questions.question_text: AgCN reacts with haloalkanes to form isocyanide. Haloalkanes react with KCN to form alkyl cyanides as the main product. Why?
questions.question_html: AgCN reacts with haloalkanes to form isocyanide. Haloalkanes react with KCN to form alkyl cyanides as the main product. Why?

options.text: KCN is mainly covalent in nature.
options.html: KCN is mainly covalent in nature.

options.text: KCN provides cyanide ions in solution.
options.html: KCN provides cyanide ions in solution.

options.text: AgCN provides cyanide ions in solution.
options.html: AgCN provides cyanide ions in solution.

options.text: AgCN is mainly ionic in nature.
options.html: AgCN is mainly ionic in nature.

questions.correct_option_text: KCN provides cyanide ions in solution.
questions.explanation: AgCN is covalent in nature while KCN is ionic. So KCN produces CN - in the solution. CN - is an ambient nucleophile i.e. it can form bonds from both C and N atoms. KCN is an ionic compound so both C and N sides are available for the formation of a bond, but the C-C bond is stronger than the C-N bond so cyanide is formed. The AgCN is a covalent compound, so only the N side is available for CN to form bond and thus isocyanide is formed.

questions.question_text: Isopropyl alcohol on oxidation forms:
questions.question_html: Isopropyl alcohol on oxidation forms:
questions.explanation: Secondary alcohols are oxidized to ketones. The oxidation of isopropyl alcohol by potassium dichromate (K 2 Cr 2 O 7 ) gives acetone, the simplest ketone. Unlike aldehydes, ketones are relatively resistant to further oxidation, so no special precautions are required to isolate them as they form.

questions.question_text: Cheilosis is caused by deficiency of
questions.question_html: Cheilosis is caused by deficiency of
questions.explanation: Deficiency of Vitamin B 2 (Riboflavin) results in Cheilosis (fissuring at corners of mouth and lips).

questions.question_text: The radius of an ion in a body centred cube of edge a is:
questions.question_html: The radius of an ion in a body centred cube of edge a is:
questions.explanation: The length of the body diagonal c is equal to 4r Therefore, {tex}\sqrt 3 a = 4r{/tex} a = {tex}\frac{4r}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex} r = {tex}\frac{{\sqrt 3 a}}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_text: Which among the following form nearly ideal solutions?
questions.question_html: Which among the following form nearly ideal solutions?
questions.explanation: The intermolecular attractive forces between benzene-benzene and toluene-toluene are nearly equal to those between benzene-toluene, this leads to the formation of ideal solution.

questions.question_text: Which compound is used in the preparation of caprolactam?
questions.question_html: Which compound is used in the preparation of caprolactam?
questions.explanation: NH 3 is used in the preparation of caprolactam. Caprolactam is an organic compound with the formula (CH 2 ) 5 C(O)NH. It is used to make Nylon 6 fibre and plastics.

questions.question_text: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.question_html: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.explanation: Liquid NH 3 is used as a refrigerant. evaporation of a liquid needs heat energy. When liquid ammonia vapourises, it absorbs large quantities of heat without changing its temperature.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compounds is aromatic alcohol?
questions.question_html: Which of the following compounds is aromatic alcohol?
questions.explanation: Compound (A) i.e., phenol and compound (D) i.e. a derivative of phenol cannot be considered as aromatic alcohol. As phenol is also known as, carbolic acid cannot be considered as aromatic alcohol. Benzyl alcohol and substituted benzyl alcohol are aromatic alcohol.

questions.question_text: The sugar constituent of DNA is
questions.question_html: The sugar constituent of DNA is
questions.explanation: Complete hydrolysis of DNA yields a pentose sugar, phosphoric acid and nitrogen containing heterocyclic compounds (called bases). In DNA molecules, the sugar moiety is β-D-2-deoxyribose.

questions.question_text: is the structure of:-
questions.question_html: is the structure of:-
questions.explanation: This structure is of H 3 PO 4 . (Orthophosphoric acid). It has three P-OH and one P=O bond.

questions.explanation: All these can be used to detect defects in solid.

questions.question_text: Addison’s disease is characterised by
questions.question_html: Addison’s disease is characterised by
questions.explanation: If adrenal cortex does not function properly then one of the results may be Addison’s disease characterised by hypoglycemia, weakness and increased susceptibility to stress.

questions.question_text: is the structure of:
questions.question_html: is the structure of:
questions.explanation: H 3 PO 2 is Hypophosphorus acid. It has one P-OH, two P-H and one P=O bonds.

questions.question_text: Elements of which group are known as ore forming elements?
questions.question_html: Elements of which group are known as ore forming elements?
questions.explanation: A maximum number of ores contain group 16 elements (O, S, etc). They are known as "chalcogens" which was derived from the Greek word chalcos, meaning "ore formers," since they all are be found in copper ores.

questions.question_text: Most metal oxides are:
questions.question_html: Most metal oxides are:
questions.explanation: In general, metal oxides are basic and ionic in nature.

questions.question_text: The following compound can be called as
questions.question_html: The following compound can be called as
questions.explanation: Glucose is correctly named as D(+)-glucose. ‘D’ before the name of glucose represents the configuration whereas ‘(+)’ represents dextrorotatory nature of the molecule. When the -OH group lies on right hand side in the structure it is said to have D configuration. In (+) glucose, -OH on the lowest asymmetric carbon is on the right side so (+) glucose is assigned D-configuration.

questions.question_html: When 3, 3-dimethyl-2-butanol is heated with concentrated H2SO4, the major product obtained is:

questions.question_text: Give IUPAC names of the following compound:
questions.question_html: Give IUPAC names of the following compound:
questions.explanation: -Cl (chloro) is given priority over -CH 3 (methyl) group. So it is numbered as 1. So the IUPAC name is 1-chloro-2 methylbenzene. Also toluene is an IUPAC accepted name for methyl benzene. Hence the given compound can be named as 2-chlorotoluene also, in which case -Cl is numbered

questions.question_text: The thermal stability of hydrides of group 16 elements decreases in the order:
questions.question_html: The thermal stability of hydrides of group 16 elements decreases in the order:

options.text: H 2 S, H 2 O H 2 Se, H 2 Te
H 2 O, H 2 S, H 2 Te, H 2 Se
H 2 S, H 2 Se, H 2 Te,H 2 O
H 2 O, H 2 S, H 2 Se, H 2 Te

questions.correct_option_text: H 2 O, H 2 S, H 2 Se, H 2 Te
questions.explanation: As we move down the group bond length increases and bond enthalpy for the dissociation of H-E bond decreases. Due to a decrease in enthalpy, the thermal stability of hydrides decreases down the group.

questions.question_text: Formula of oleum is
questions.question_html: Formula of oleum is
questions.explanation: Oleum is pyrosulphuric acid (H 2 S 2 O 7 )

questions.question_text: Silicon carbide is an example of
questions.question_html: Silicon carbide is an example of
questions.explanation: SiC has covalent bonding, It is hard and an insulator.

questions.question_text: What is the correct order of reactivity of alcohols in the following reaction? {tex}R - OH + HCl\xrightarrow{{ZnC{l_2}}}R - Cl + {H_2}O{/tex}
questions.question_html:
What is the correct order of reactivity of alcohols in the following reaction?

{tex}R - OH + HCl\xrightarrow{{ZnC{l_2}}}R - Cl + {H_2}O{/tex}
questions.explanation: The mixture of HCl and ZnCl 2 is called the Lucas Reagent. Secondary and tertiary alcohols react via the S N 1 mechanism with the Lucas reagent. The ZnCl 2 coordinates to the hydroxyl oxygen and this generates a far superior leaving group. When alcohols react with a hydrogen halide, a substitution occurs, producing an alkyl halide and water: Scope of Reaction: The order of reactivity of alcohols is 3° > 2° > 1°

questions.question_text: Graphite cannot be classified as ________.
questions.question_html: Graphite cannot be classified as ________.
questions.explanation: Graphite can't be classified as ionic solid as graphite is not made up of ions. It is made up of carbon atoms covalently bonded to three of its neighbouring carbon atoms in the same layer so, it is a covalent solid. Since the formation of a covalent bond occurs throughout the crystal therefore, it is a type of network solid. Due to the presence of free electron graphite is also classified as conductor solid.

questions.question_text: The synthesis of 3 – octyne is achieved by adding a bromoalkane into a mixture of sodium amide and an alkyne. The bromoalkane and alkyne respectively are
questions.question_html: The synthesis of 3 – octyne is achieved by adding a bromoalkane into a mixture of sodium amide and an alkyne. The bromoalkane and alkyne respectively are

options.text: BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH

questions.correct_option_text: BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
questions.explanation: CH 3 CH 2 C {tex} \equiv {/tex} BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH3+NaNH2 {tex} \to {/tex} CH3CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 C {tex} \equiv {/tex} CH+NH 3 +NaBr NaNH 2 is a very strong base it will abstract H from alkyne (But-1-yne in this case) and then nucleophilic substitution reaction will occur as shown where the resulting compound will be Oct-1-yne as here the reactants contain a total of 8 C.

questions.question_text: Which among the following does not exhibit a positive oxidation state?
questions.question_html: Which among the following does not exhibit a positive oxidation state?
questions.explanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative element, it shows only -1 oxidation state.

questions.question_text: The preparation of ethers by acid dehydration of secondary or tertiary alcohols is not a suitable method. This is so because:
questions.question_html: The preparation of ethers by acid dehydration of secondary or tertiary alcohols is not a suitable method. This is so because:

options.text: Elimination competes over substitution and alkenes are easily formed.
options.html: Elimination competes over substitution and alkenes are easily formed.

questions.correct_option_text: Elimination competes over substitution and alkenes are easily formed.
questions.explanation: The formation of ethers by dehydration of the alcohol is a bimolecular reaction (S N 2 ) involving the attack of an alcohol molecule on a protonated alcohol molecule. In the method, the alkyl group should be unhindered. In the case of secondary or tertiary alcohols, the alkyl group is hindered. As a result, elimination dominates substitution as 3° carbocation is more stable. Hence, in place of ethers, alkenes are formed.

questions.question_text: Fluorine reacts with H 2 S to produce
questions.question_html: Fluorine reacts with H2S to produce
questions.explanation: 4F 2 + H 2 S {tex}\to{/tex} SF 6 + 2HF

questions.question_text: DNA and RNA contain four bases each. Which of the following bases is not present in RNA?
questions.question_html: DNA and RNA contain four bases each. Which of the following bases is not present in RNA?
questions.explanation: DNA contains four bases viz. adenine (A), guanine (G), cytosine (C) and thymine (T). RNA also contains four bases, the first three bases are same as in DNA but the fourth one is uracil (U).

Reason (R): Free electrons are spread out in the structure of graphite.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Vinylic halides are highly reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reactions. Reason (R): Reactivity is due to the polarity of carbon-halogen bond.
questions.question_html:
Assertion (A): Vinylic halides are highly reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reactions.
Reason (R): Reactivity is due to the polarity of carbon-halogen bond.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
options.html: A is true but R is false.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.html: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
options.html: A is false but R is true.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.html: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.correct_option_text: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: Vinylic halides are very less reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reactions because of the resonance effect. Resonance gives rise to partial double bond character to the carbon-halogen bond making it stronger and therefore more difficult to cleave than a C sp 3 - X bond. It also reduces the polarity of the carbon-halogen bond thereby making heterolysis difficult.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Glucose when treated with CH 3 OH in presence of dry HCl gas gives {tex}\alpha{/tex} -and {tex}\beta{/tex} -methyl glucosides. Reason (R): Glucose reacts with phenylhydrazine to form crystalline osazone.

Reason (R): Glucose reacts with phenylhydrazine to form crystalline osazone.

questions.correct_option_text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: Because of the ring structure, C 1 in glucose becomes chiral and hence glucose exists in two stereoisomeric forms, i.e., {tex}\alpha{/tex} - and {tex}\beta{/tex} -, corresponding to each stereoisomeric form glucose forms two methyl glucosides, i.e., {tex}\alpha{/tex} -and {tex}\beta{/tex} -methyl glucosides.

questions.question_text: Liver when chronically exposed to chloroform gets damaged because:
questions.question_html: Liver when chronically exposed to chloroform gets damaged because:

options.text: chloroform depresses the central nervous system.
options.html: chloroform depresses the central nervous system.

options.html: chloroform develops sores in the liver.

questions.correct_option_text: chloroform gets converted to carbonyl chloride which is a poison.
questions.explanation: Chronic chloroform exposure may cause damage to the liver (where chloroform is metabolized to phosgene) CHCl 3 + O 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2COCl 2 + 2HCl

questions.question_text: C 6 H 5 OCH 2 CH 3 is called:
questions.question_html: C6H5OCH2CH3 is called:
questions.explanation: C 6 H 5 OCH 2 CH 3 is ethyl phenyl ether or phenetole is an organic compound that is ether. Ethyl phenyl ether has the same properties as some other ethers, such as volatility, explosive vapors, and the ability to form peroxides. IUPAC name : Ethoxybenzene Other names : Phenetole, Ethyl Phenyl Ether

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: At 298 K, the vapour pressure of pure benzene, C 6 H 6 is 0.256 bar and the vapour pressure of pure toluene C 6 H 5 CH 3 is 0.0925 bar. Two mixtures were prepared as follows: 7.8 g of C 6 H 6 + 9.2 g of toluene 3.9 g of C 6 H 6 + 13.8 g of toluene The total vapour pressure (bar) of solution I is:
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

At 298 K, the vapour pressure of pure benzene, C6 H6 is 0.256 bar and the vapour pressure of pure toluene C6H5CH3 is 0.0925 bar. Two mixtures were prepared as follows:

  1. 7.8 g of C6H6 + 9.2 g of toluene
  2. 3.9 g of C6H6 + 13.8 g of toluene

The total vapour pressure (bar) of solution I is:
questions.explanation: Moles of C 6 H 6 = {tex}\frac{7.8}{78}{/tex} = 0.1 Moles of C 6 H 5 CH 3 = {tex}\frac{9.2}{92}{/tex} = 0.1 Mole fraction of C 6 H 6 = {tex}\frac{0.1}{0.1 + 0.1}{/tex} = 0.5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Mole fraction of C 6 H 5 CH 3 = 0.5 Vapour pressure of toluene = Vapour pressure of pure toluene {tex}\times{/tex} mole fraction of toluene = 0.0925 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5 = 0.04625 Vapour pressure of benzene = 0.256 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5 = 0.128 Total vapour pressure of solution = 0.17425

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: At 298 K, the vapour pressure of pure benzene, C 6 H 6 is 0.256 bar and the vapour pressure of pure toluene C 6 H 5 CH 3 is 0.0925 bar. Two mixtures were prepared as follows: 7.8 g of C 6 H 6 + 9.2 g of toluene 3.9 g of C 6 H 6 + 13.8 g of toluene Which of the given solutions have higher vapour pressure?
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

At 298 K, the vapour pressure of pure benzene, C6 H6 is 0.256 bar and the vapour pressure of pure toluene C6H5CH3 is 0.0925 bar. Two mixtures were prepared as follows:

  1. 7.8 g of C6H6 + 9.2 g of toluene
  2. 3.9 g of C6H6 + 13.8 g of toluene

Which of the given solutions have higher vapour pressure?
questions.explanation: Moles of benzene in solution-II = {tex}\frac{3.9}{78}{/tex} = 0.5 Vapour pressure of solution = 0.256 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.05 + 0.0925 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.15 = 0.0128 + 0.013875 = 0.026675

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: At 298 K, the vapour pressure of pure benzene, C 6 H 6 is 0.256 bar and the vapour pressure of pure toluene C 6 H 5 CH 3 is 0.0925 bar. Two mixtures were prepared as follows: 7.8 g of C 6 H 6 + 9.2 g of toluene 3.9 g of C 6 H 6 + 13.8 g of toluene Mole fraction of benzene in vapour phase in solution I is
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

At 298 K, the vapour pressure of pure benzene, C6 H6 is 0.256 bar and the vapour pressure of pure toluene C6H5CH3 is 0.0925 bar. Two mixtures were prepared as follows:

  1. 7.8 g of C6H6 + 9.2 g of toluene
  2. 3.9 g of C6H6 + 13.8 g of toluene

Mole fraction of benzene in vapour phase in solution I is
questions.explanation: Mole fraction of benzene in vapour phase y benzene = {tex}\frac{P_{ {benzene }}}{P_{ {total }}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{0.128}{0.17425}{/tex} = 0.734
